About Me

Open Street Map Mapper

Passionate about
Geospatial Worlds

I am a Geomatics Engineering student at Kathmandu University with a big interest in everything related to Geospatial technology. Besides my technical studies, I have a lot of experience in managing events and moderating programs through different student clubs.

I also worked as a magazine editor, which helped me learn how to organize content and communicate ideas clearly. I am hardworking, organized, and always excited to learn new things in the GIS field.

My hands-on work with UAV mapping, Pix4D post-processing, and tools like QGIS has given me real practical knowledge of how geospatial data is collected, processed, and turned into meaningful products like orthophotos and elevation models.

Experience

What I've Done

2025 – Present
Vice Editor-in-Chief
GeoSpace Magazine Vol X

Served as Vice Editor-in-Chief for the Annual Magazine 2025. Coordinated with editors, writers, and designers to prepare the magazine. Involved in reviewing and editing articles to improve clarity and overall quality. Helped in planning the magazine structure and organizing contents. Made sure tasks were completed on time and supported the team throughout the publication process.

Design Work
Design Experience
Figma & Canva

Have experience using Figma and Canva for designing resumes, posters, presentations, and other academic and club-related materials. Focus on keeping the design simple, well-organized, and easy to read. Pay attention to layout, alignment, and text arrangement to make the content look clean and professional. Worked with friends and classmates while preparing designs for different activities and programs.

Field Work
UAV Mapping & Pix4D Post-Processing
Geomatics Field Project

Have experience working on UAV mapping activities with senior members. Involved in basic flight planning and data collection using drones. After data acquisition, assisted in post-processing aerial images in Pix4D to generate orthophotos and elevation products. Learned the basics of image processing, data accuracy, and teamwork, which helped gain practical knowledge of UAV-based mapping.

Who Inspires Me

My Guiding Lights

1920 – 1972
Nation Builder · 9th King of Nepal

King Mahendra Bir Bikram Shah

The ninth King of Nepal (1955–1972), King Mahendra was a bold visionary who transformed a feudal nation into a modernizing state. He opened Nepal to the outside world after 104 years of Rana isolationism, built the first highways and national infrastructure, and passionately promoted the Nepali language and culture. A poet writing under the name "Mahendra Malla," he combined political will with cultural pride. His unwavering ambition to build a unified, self-reliant Nepal inspires me to think big and act with conviction.

"A nation's strength lies in the unity and progress of its people."
563 – 483 BCE
Philosopher · The Enlightened One

Gautam Buddha

Born in Lumbini, Nepal — Siddhartha Gautama renounced a life of royal comfort to seek the truth of human suffering and liberation. Through meditation, mindfulness, and the Middle Path, he achieved enlightenment and shared his wisdom with the world. The Buddha teaches me that clarity of mind, compassion for others, and patience in the face of difficulty are the foundations of any meaningful work. As a student and mapmaker of the earth, his teachings remind me to remain grounded, humble, and purposeful in everything I do.

"Peace comes from within. Do not seek it without."
The Eternal · Mahadev
Deity · The Destroyer & Creator

Lord Shiva

Lord Shiva — the Mahadev — embodies the cosmic dance of creation and destruction, of absolute stillness and infinite power. He is the lord of mountains (the Himalayas are his eternal abode), the patron of yogis, and the force that destroys what must end so something greater can arise. As someone from the mountain region of Rukum, working in a field that studies the very earth itself, I feel a deep spiritual connection to Shiva. His unwavering focus, freedom from ego, and courage to transform inspire me in both life and study.

"In stillness lies infinite power. In surrender, ultimate strength."
Born 1960 · Palpa, Nepal
Cardiac Surgeon · National Hero

Dr. Bhagwan Koirala

Born in rural Palpa, Dr. Bhagwan Koirala rose to become Nepal's most celebrated cardiac surgeon. In 1997, he led the first open-heart surgery by an all-Nepali team, and in 2016 performed Nepal's first heart transplant. He transformed an abandoned shoe warehouse into Shahid Gangalal National Heart Centre — now serving 300,000+ patients annually with over 14,000 cardiovascular surgeries performed. Despite lucrative offers abroad, he chose to serve Nepal. His story proves that your origin is never a limit, and inspires me to use every skill I build in service of my country.

"Return to your roots. There is no greater mission than serving the land that made you."
